instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Term Insurance Claim Rules
What Is a Term Insurance Claim?
A term insurance plan covers the risk of premature death or demise during the policy tenure. Moreover, if you choose the return of premium policy, you get your premiums refunded if the plan matures and you survive the tenure.
When the event insured under the term plan occurs, there’s a claim. A term insurance claim is the demand that you make on the insurance company to pay the promised benefit. A term insurance claim usually occurs in the following instances –
If the insured dies during the policy tenure – In this case, a death claim is incurred
If the insured survives the policy tenure – in this case, there’s a maturity claim applicable in return of premium term plans wherein the premiums are refunded
Step-By-Step Process of Term Insurance Claim Settlement
In the case of a death claim, you have to follow the term life insurance claim process to get the claim settled. The process depends on the insurance company but it is usually similar. Some of the common steps in the term insurance claim procedure are as follows1 –
Report the claim
This is the first step in the term plan claim process wherein the insurance company is informed of the death of the life insured and a subsequent claim. under this process, the claimant is required to fill out a death claim form and submit it with the relevant documents to the insurance company.
The death claim form is available online on the insurer’s website and can be downloaded from there. Alternatively, you can also visit the insurers nearest branch office and get a physical form.
Claim processing
This is the second step in the term insurance claim process. After you submit the claim form and the relevant documents, the insurance company assess and verifies the submitted documents. If any additional document is needed, the insurer informs the claimant who is required to provide the documents to proceed with the term life insurance claim process. Insurers also keep the claim status updated on their website and send regular alerts to the policyholders about the same. You can also check the claim status online, through the branch office or through the insurer’s claim helpline.
Claim settlement
Once the documents are verified and processed, the insurer would either settle the claim and pay the death benefit or reject the claim. in the case of rejection, the reason for the same would be provided. If the insurance company wants to conduct investigations into the death of the insured, the same would be conducted and then the claim would be settled. In order to avoid rejections, it is important to disclose all relevant facts to the insurer during application.
Important Inclusions and Exclusions
Term insurance plans cover premature demise occurring within the chosen policy tenure. However, the plan specifies the types of deaths it would cover and the types it wouldn’t. some of the common inclusions and exclusions in term insurance plans are as follows2 –
Documents Required for Term Insurance Claim Submission
As mentioned earlier, when reporting your term insurance claim, you have to submit specific documents to the insurance company. Some of these documents are as follows1 -
The original policy document
Attested copy of the death certificate issued by the relevant authorities
The death claim form was filled and signed by the claimant
Copy of cancelled cheque or NEFT mandate form attested by the bank wherein you want to receive the death payout
Identity and address proof of the nominee like the passport, Aadhaar Card, Voter’s ID Card, etc.
In the case of natural or medical deaths, the following additional documents are also required1 –
Medical records of the insured which include the test reports, admission report, discharge or death summary, etc.
Statement from the attending physician
Certificate issued by the employer wherever applicable
In the case of accidental deaths, the following additional documents are needed6 –
Police FIR or panchnama
Post Mortem Report (PMR)
Final investigation report issued by the police
Considerations While Opting for Term Insurance Claim
When making a claim in your term insurance policy, here are some factors to keep in mind –
Check whether the policy is active or in a lapsed state. If your term plan is lapsed, chances are that the coverage will not be active. In such cases, the claim might get rejected.
See if the death is covered under the scope of the policy.
If you have selected any rider and are eligible for the rider’s benefit, inform the insurer about the same when making a claim.
Keep the nominee informed of the term insurance claim process. Also, inform the nominee of all the policies that you have so that in your absence the nominee can raise a claim from all the plans.
Conditions of Ensuring the Settlement of Term Insurance Claim
To ensure a smooth and hassle-free settlement of term insurance claim, here are some factors that you can keep in mind –
Fill out the claim form accurately and completely. Double-check the information entered to avoid any confusion in claim settlement.
Keep all the claim-related documents handy and in one place. If the insurance company asks for additional documents, arrange them on priority and submit them for quick settlements.
In the case of a claim-related dispute, find out the reason behind the same and try to address the concerns that the insurance company might have.
Intimate the claim at the earliest. If there’s a considerable delay in the time the insured died and the time that the claim is lodged, there might be hassles in claim settlement.
Ensure the documents are all genuine and attested as per the insurer’s needs
Conclusion
Insurance companies have greatly simplified the term insurance claim procedure to allow policyholders to get quick financial assistance in times when they need it the most. However, you have to do your part and follow the specified term life insurance claim process to get your claim settled. Know the process, maintain a checklist of the documents required and cooperate with the insurance company for quick and smooth claim settlements.
FAQs
How long does it take to receive the claim?
The claim settlement time varies across companies and depends on whether the claim process has been rightly followed and the documents duly submitted. If the process and documentation are correct, you can get the claim within the shortest possible time.
What to do if there is a delay in insurance claim settlement?
You can raise a complaint with the insurance company if there’s a delay in claim settlement. If the insurance company does not solve your grievance, you can go to the insurance Ombudsman and demand quick claim settlements.
What happens if the policy premiums are not paid?
If the policy premiums are not paid, the term insurance coverage lapses. Usually, in pure protection term insurance plans, if you do not revive the lapsed policy, no benefit is paid if the insured dies during the policy tenure. However, if you have chosen the return of premium plan, you might get a reduced paid-up value if you discontinue premium payments after paying a minimum number of premiums.
Reference
https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-claim-assistance.html
https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/term/types-of-death-covered-and-not-covered-by-term-insurance.html
Disclaimer
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions.
